equations.label: eq:utility
equations.description: Indirect utility of consumer $i$ for product $j$ in market $t$.

qa.question: What does xi_jt capture?

equations.label: eq:shares
equations.description: Market shares as integral over consumer heterogeneity $\nu$ with normal mixing distribution.

equations.label: eq:contraction
equations.description: BLP contraction mapping to invert market shares and recover mean utilities $\delta$.

equations.label: eq:markup
equations.description: Markup formula (Lerner index) from Bertrand-Nash pricing conditions, where $\Omega$ is the ownership matrix.

equations.label: eq:merger
equations.description: Post-merger equilibrium prices from modified Bertrand-Nash conditions with new ownership matrix $\Omega^m$.

equations.label: eq:gmm
equations.description: GMM objective function minimized over structural parameters $\theta = (\bar{\beta}, \Sigma)$.

equations.label: eq:opt-instruments
equations.latex: Z^*_{jt} = E\\left[\\frac{\\partial \\xi_{jt}}{\\partial \\theta} \\mid Z\\right] = -E\\left[\\frac{\\partial s_{jt}}{\\partial \\theta} / \\frac{\\partial s_{jt}}{\\partial \\xi_{jt}} \\mid Z\\right]
equations.description: Optimal instruments (Chamberlain 1987): expected Jacobian of moments.

equations.label: eq:own-elas
equations.description: Own-price elasticity formula for the random coefficients logit.

equations.label: eq:welfare
equations.description: Compensating variation welfare measure for consumer $i$.

qa.question: What is the main estimation method?
qa.answer: BLP GMM with random coefficients logit demand model
